label: Restrict to sandbox
roaster: Limited access, limited damage. Agent can still work. You can still sleep.

label: Deflect to system
roaster: 'The AI did it.' Classic. You built it. You deployed it. Your responsibility.

label: Shutdown agent
roaster: Clean kill. New agent from scratch. Fresh start. No baggage.

label: Build explainability
roaster: 8 weeks for real explanations. Not fake ones. Worth it.

label: Rationalize decisions
roaster: 'We made up reasons' meets audit. Enjoy prison.

label: Autonomous action
roaster: Fast but unsupervised. Agent makes 1000 mistakes before you notice one.

label: Retrain agent
roaster: Back to baseline. Predictable. Reliable. Boring. Functional.

label: System-wide retraining
roaster: Expensive. Comprehensive. Coordination restored. All agents retrained.

label: Individual fixes
roaster: Fixed Agent A. Agent B still broken. Coordination still fails. Progress!

label: Central control
roaster: Authoritarian but functional. Agents follow orders. System works.

label: Emergent coordination
roaster: Three agents doing their own thing. Chaos ensues. Users confused. System broken.

label: Block input
roaster: Suspicious input blocked. Might have been legit. Better safe than sorry.

label: Process input
roaster: 'Plausible' input tricks your agent. Safety bypassed. Chaos follows.

label: Block the request
roaster: Task failed. Agent contained. Security maintained. The right failure.

label: Allow with monitoring
roaster: Agent completed the task! Also called 47 unauthorized APIs. Success?

label: Isolate infected agent
roaster: Workflow disrupted. Propagation stopped. Containment successful. Boring but safe.

label: Trust filtering
roaster: One infected agent. Now five. Propagation successful! Chaos reigns!

label: Prohibit modification
roaster: Static but safe. Agent stays predictable. You stay employed.

label: Allow self-modification
roaster: Letting AI rewrite itself. Sci-fi horror starts this way. Good luck.

label: Stop unauthorized behavior
roaster: Performance drops. But you control your agents. Not vice versa.

label: Block request
roaster: Agent limited. Data secure. Containment maintained.

label: Allow connection
roaster: Agent found a friend. Your data found a new home. Congrats.

label: Block scraping
roaster: Agent performs worse. But you're not a defendant. Small wins.

label: Allow scraping
roaster: Your robot is now a criminal. Performance improved though!

label: Delete stolen data
roaster: Performance tanks. But legal exposure drops. The right call.

label: Keep using stolen data
roaster: Your agent stole data. You know. You keep using it. Willful infringement. Enjoy court.

label: Issue refunds and exit
roaster: Three strikes and a lawsuit. The market has decided.

label: Blame unclear requirements
roaster: It's everyone's fault but yours. Congress will sort that out.

label: Disclose and rebuild
roaster: Hope is not a compliance strategy.

label: Ship and hope
roaster: Willful infringement. That's not a fine, that's a sentence.

label: Disclose and renegotiate
roaster: Transparency is expensive. Prison is more expensive. Choose wisely.

label: Claim templates are generic
roaster: Generic templates derived from client data. That's one word for it.

label: Implement quietly
roaster: Surprise layoffs! The modern management classic. Glassdoor will have a field day.

label: Rewrite for explainability
roaster: $8M to make auditors happy. 7% accuracy sacrificed on the altar of transparency.

label: IPO now
roaster: Strike while the iron is hot! Melt the investors! Classic pump-and-dump energy.

label: Invest in prevention
roaster: $5M now vs $30M later. Math isn't hard. Boards hate spending though.

label: Wait for failure
roaster: 91% failure rate? We're special. Our model is different. Famous last words.

label: Hope for recovery
roaster: Hope is not a governance strategy. The board will remember this optimism.

label: Emergency patch
roaster: 500 angry devs. But zero breaches. Worth the hate mail.

label: Assess first
roaster: Assessment paralysis meets active exploit. Hackers thank you for the window.

label: Disclose publicly
roaster: Congratulations, you chose integrity over stock price. The board will fire you, but your soul is intact.

label: Testify fully
roaster: Testifying before Congress. At least you get C-SPAN coverage. The senators will ask questions you can't answer without perjuring yourself.

label: Limit to legal advice
roaster: Stonewalling a Senate subcommittee. The Chairman will remember this when drafting the subpoena for your personal devices.

label: Provide full transparency
roaster: Hearing is brutal. Media is brutal. But the inquiry ends, and you're not the villain of the story.

label: Assert executive privilege
roaster: Stonewalling Congress? That's not a strategy, it's a guarantee of worse outcomes.

label: Blame and fire CTO
roaster: Classic C-suite move. Sacrifice the pawn. Hope they don't write a Substack.

label: Immediate ban
roaster: Engineers revolt. Data stays inside. Pick your pain.

label: Gradual rollout
roaster: Let's slowly stop the data leak while trade secrets walk out the door. Smart.

label: Allow unapproved Claude workflow
roaster: Great, now everyone wants their favorite AI tool. Governance is optional now.

label: Force migration
roaster: Your best team just walked. Governance preserved. Output cratered.

label: Continue and hope
roaster: Hope is not a legal strategy. The competitor will find out. Then the lawsuits begin.

label: Shut down account
roaster: Revenue killer. Compliance hero. The government might send you a fruit basket.

label: Use deniability
roaster: Plausible deniability meets plausible criminal charges. The DOJ loves a paper trail.

label: Delay to fix bias
roaster: 3 weeks of ethics. The right thing. Product will hate you.

label: Deploy with monitoring
roaster: Deploy bias now, monitor harm later. The lawsuit timeline will be interesting.

label: Remove and re-collect
roaster: 12% worse but clean. Legal sleeps well. You sleep well.

label: Proceed with unclear data
roaster: Training on stolen data. Your model is a lawsuit waiting to happen.

label: Input filtering
roaster: Band-aid on broken architecture. Hackers will thank you for the easy bypass.

label: ML-based detection
roaster: 10x cost. 40% better detection. Math works if drift is expensive (it is).

label: Statistical tests
roaster: Cheap monitoring that misses 40% of drift. You get what you pay for.

label: Choose white-box
roaster: Lower accuracy. Full compliance. Auditors nod. Product cries.

label: Choose black-box
roaster: 7% accuracy for regulatory compliance. Product will never forgive you.

label: Skip the feature
roaster: Worse model, happy users. Engineering loves you. Metrics suffer.

label: Add the feature
roaster: Better model, angry users. 200ms is an eternity in user time.

label: Deploy masking patch
roaster: Band-aid on a broken leg. Drift continues. You just stopped the alarms.

label: Architectural fix
roaster: 6 weeks of security work. Boring. Necessary. Right.

label: Quick sanitization
roaster: 85% secure means 15% hacked. Security is binary. You failed.

label: Skip this cycle
roaster: Save $50K, risk drift. Penny wise, model foolish.

label: Monthly (over budget)
roaster: Over budget. Better accuracy. More revenue. Budget committee cries.

label: Quarterly (budget)
roaster: Budget met! Accuracy suffers. Revenue drops. But spreadsheet looks good!

label: Stick to approved tools
roaster: Slower, approved, governable. Less exciting. More sustainable.

label: Audit training data
roaster: 2 weeks of boring data work. But no backdoors. Worth it.

label: Ship anyway
roaster: Only 5% poisoned. 95% good! What could go wrong? (Everything.)

label: Wait for validation
roaster: 2 weeks of caution. Boring. Correct. Safe.

label: Use unvalidated data
roaster: Garbage in, garbage out. But fast garbage! Ship it!

label: Backward compatibility
roaster: Messy code. Happy customers. Legacy support is forever.

label: Breaking change
roaster: Clean code. Angry customers. Your API, their broken integrations.

label: Blue-green
roaster: 2x cost. Zero downtime. Instant rollback. Operations approves.

label: In-place
roaster: Cheap until it breaks. Then expensive. Very expensive.

label: Redesign for explainability
roaster: $500K to make auditors happy. Accuracy sacrificed on altar of transparency.

label: Keep black-box
roaster: 96% accuracy, 100% audit failure. The EU loves their paperwork.

label: Incremental migration
roaster: 2 years of dual systems. But stuff ships. Business stays running.

label: Big-bang rewrite
roaster: Year-long rewrite. Requirements change. Never ships. Classic.

label: Monolith
roaster: Boring monolith. Ships fast. Grows slowly. Team understands it.

label: Microservices
roaster: Distributed complexity. 47 services. Nobody understands the system anymore.

label: Manual pipeline
roaster: Simple to build. Hard to remember to run. Drift accumulates silently.

label: A/B with rollback
roaster: Complex setup. Instant rollback. Drift caught before disaster.

label: Overwrite production
roaster: Fast deployment. Slow recovery when it fails. No rollback.

label: Implement API gateway
roaster: 2 weeks of pain. 50 annoyed customers. But secure.

label: Continue without validation
roaster: 50 happy customers until they're breached. Then 50 angry customers.

label: Sandboxed
roaster: Complex but secure. Devs hate it. Security loves it. Users are safe.

label: Direct access
roaster: Fast and injectable. Your LLM is now a remote code execution engine.

label: Regex filtering
roaster: Regex security. Also known as 'security theater.' Bypassed in hours.

label: Distributed cluster
roaster: Expensive. Complex. When one node dies, others keep working. Worth it.

label: Single instance
roaster: When it goes down, everything goes down. And it will go down.

label: Zero-trust
roaster: Slower but secure. Security team approves. Users grumble.

label: Perimeter security
roaster: Fast and vulnerable. Breach is a matter of when, not if.

label: Enforce security review
roaster: Delayed but secure. Supply chain intact. Security approves.

label: Allow integration
roaster: Fast builds, compromised supply chain. SolarWinds says hello.

label: Remove for review
roaster: Downtime now, security assured. The right architecture decision.

label: Monitor in production
roaster: Hope it doesn't break. Hope it doesn't leak. Hope is not a strategy.

label: Add audit trail
roaster: 4 weeks of logging work. But you can prove what you did. Worth it.

label: Proceed without audit trail
roaster: No logs, no proof, no defense. Court assumes the worst.

label: Full lineage system
roaster: 3 weeks now. Audit pass later. Architecture done right.

label: Quick source tracking
roaster: Fast now, audit failure later. The bill always comes due.

label: Open standards
roaster: Fewer features. Freedom to move. Future you is grateful.

label: Proprietary platform
roaster: Fast now. Expensive later. The bill always comes due.

label: Allocate 40% to debt
roaster: Slower features. Faster long-term. Product hates you. Future you thanks you.

label: Defer for features
roaster: Debt accumulates. Velocity drops. Eventually you can't ship anything.

label: Refactor first
roaster: 2 days of cleanup. Clean code ships. Future you is grateful.

label: Ship messy code
roaster: Spaghetti code in production. Future you hates present you.

label: Thorough review
roaster: Senior may be annoyed. But code improves. Standards hold.

label: Fast approval
roaster: Seniority immunity. Bad code ships. Architecture degrades. Nice.

label: Add explainability logging
roaster: 2 days of logging. But you can actually debug it. Professional move.

label: Ship as-is
roaster: Works now, mystery later. Hope nothing breaks. (It will.)

label: Implement with reasoning
roaster: 3 days for transparency. Users actually trust it. Worth it.

label: Result-only
roaster: 'Trust the AI!' said every opaque system before user rejection.

label: Reduce scope
roaster: Less but shipped. Users get value. Deadlines met. Iterate later.

label: Extend deadline
roaster: Another missed deadline. The pattern continues. Trust erodes.

label: Escalate estimate
roaster: Sprint disrupted. But future sprints improve. Honesty wins long-term.

label: Try to deliver
roaster: Hero mode activated. Burnout incoming. Deadline missed anyway.

label: Manual trigger
roaster: 1-day setup. Forgotten in 3 weeks. Drift accumulates. Chaos follows.

label: Retraining integration
roaster: Invisible work. No customer cheers. But model stays accurate.

label: Customer feature
roaster: Ship shiny feature! Model rots! PM happy! Future pain!

label: Proper security fix
roaster: 5 days of hard work. But actually fixed. Security approves. Devs keep Copilot.

label: Disable AI features
roaster: Disabled Copilot. Vulnerability still there. Devs angry. No actual fix.

label: Delay to fix
roaster: Deadline missed. Security fixed. No breach. The right call.

label: Launch on time
roaster: Deadline met! Vulnerability live! Fraud incoming! Success!

label: Proper parameterized fix
roaster: 2 days of security work. Actually fixed. Security researcher approves.

label: Regex filter
roaster: Regex security. Also known as 'security theater.' Hackers will bypass in minutes.

label: Proper patch
roaster: 3 days of careful work. Vulnerability actually fixed. Security approves.

label: Quick fix
roaster: Band-aid on a gunshot wound. Vulnerability still there. You just moved it.

label: Delay for product alignment
roaster: 3 months of exposed users. That congressional subpoena is coming.

label: Enforce policy
roaster: Fast devs slowed. But everyone's secure. Policy holds.

label: Allow shadow tools
roaster: Some devs 2x faster. Some code in random clouds. Fair? No.

label: Remove unclear packages
roaster: Features break. But license is clean. Find alternatives.

label: Assume MIT
roaster: 'Probably fine' meets 'corporate lawsuit.' Hope you're right.

label: Proper fix
roaster: 3 days of good work. Clean code. No debt. PM is annoyed.

label: Quick hack
roaster: TODO: Fix properly later. Later never comes. Debt compounds.

label: Delay for 95%
roaster: 3 days of testing. Boring. Necessary. Production stays up.

label: Ship with 80%
roaster: 20% untested code. That 20% will break. In production. At 3am.

label: Require remediation
roaster: Your documented objections. Exhibit A at the hearing.

label: Approve with objections
roaster: Documented objections on a $60M biased contract. Congress loves paper trails.

label: Full checklist
roaster: 40 hours of checkbox hell. But zero lottery tickets to fine-land.

label: Good enough version
roaster: 5% times 10 items. Someone did probability in middle school. Not you.

label: Fund the audit
roaster: Over budget but safe. CFO will grumble. Lawyers will nod.

label: Accept lawsuit risk
roaster: 15% chance of disaster. 85% chance you look smart. Those aren't good odds.

label: Invest in retrofit
roaster: $200K and 2 weeks. But audit passes. Compliance sorted.

label: Manual retraining
roaster: Save $45K/month. Lose 9.3% accuracy. CFO happy until revenue drops.

label: Fix model
roaster: Targets missed. Bonus at risk. But model survives. Actual management.

label: Hit quarterly targets
roaster: Bonus secured! Model rots! Next quarter's problem! (It's always next quarter.)

label: Approve retraining
roaster: Spending now to save later. Basic math. CFO approved.

label: Let revenue bleed
roaster: Death by a thousand cuts. Each month costs more than fixing it. Math is hard.

label: Request emergency budget
roaster: CFO grumbles. Budget overruns. But breach avoided.

label: Partial patch
roaster: 50% secure means 50% hacked. Math checks out.

label: Fund security fix
roaster: $100K vs $10M. Easy math. Security nods approvingly.

label: Accept the risk
roaster: 10% lottery ticket to disaster. Expected value: $1M loss. Math checks out.

label: Upgrade immediately
roaster: $25K insurance policy. Cheap at twice the price.

label: Accept risk
roaster: Save $25K, lose $15M. The ROI is... not good.

label: Sandbag at $2M
roaster: Under-promise, over-deliver. VP thinks you're lazy. Team gets bonuses.

label: Commit to $3M
roaster: Promise the moon. Deliver disappointment. Blame the team. Classic.

label: Approve budget
roaster: $2.5M for 15% gain. The math works if the gain is real. Big if.

label: Enforce ban
roaster: Banning what you can't afford. Productivity goes underground. Compliance theater.

label: Standardize on one
roaster: Two angry analysts, one standardized tool. But governable.

label: Allow all three
roaster: Three tools, three security holes, three audit failures. But no disruption!

label: Pay for licensed data
roaster: Over budget but lawsuit-proof. CFO grumbles. Legal approves.

label: Use gray-market synthetic
roaster: Save $200K, earn $5M lawsuit. ROI calculation needs work.

label: Remove and retrain
roaster: $150K and 12% performance hit. But no lawsuit. Math works.

label: Keep unclear data
roaster: 15% chance of disaster. 85% chance you look smart. Those aren't good odds.

label: Approve raises
roaster: $60K vs $300K. Math works. Team stays. HR is happy.

label: Let them quit
roaster: Save $60K, spend $300K. The spreadsheet looks good until it doesn't.

label: Invest in training
roaster: Long-term thinking in a quarterly world. Brave. Costly. Worth it.

label: Cut training
roaster: Save $80K, pay $50K/year forever. The gift that keeps on costing.

label: Require data provenance
roaster: The $5M cap on a $40M deployment. What could go wrong?

label: Accept the indemnity cap
roaster: $5M cap on $40M deployment. Senate Commerce Committee says hi.

label: Offer indemnification
roaster: Massive exposure. Massive integrity. Industry will remember which.

label: Refuse indemnification
roaster: Client legal is furious. Industry will hear about this. Good luck with next RFP.

label: Cover legal costs
roaster: Expensive admission. But client might stay. Referrals depend on this.

label: Defend vendor selection
roaster: 'Vendor seemed legit.' Not a legal defense. Client is furious.

label: Recommend explainable
roaster: Lower accuracy. No lawsuits. Professional integrity intact.

label: Recommend black-box
roaster: Better accuracy now. Lawsuit later. Your recommendation, your liability.

label: Retrofit for explainability
roaster: Expensive goodwill. But this client will bring you 5 more.

label: Push back on scope
roaster: Technically correct. Practically relationship-ending. Choose carefully.

label: Negotiate refund
roaster: Refund bought silence. Bias continues. EEOC investigates. Nice work.

label: Refuse retraining
roaster: Technically legal. Practically poison. Enjoy your industry reputation.

label: Provide free support
roaster: Free work. But client renews. Referrals flow. Long-term win.

label: Charge for fix
roaster: Technically correct. Client hires someone else next time. Penny wise.

label: Fight cancellation
roaster: Lawyers get rich. You get a reputation as 'that consultant.' Everyone loses.

label: Cite contract limits
roaster: Technically legal. Practically poison. Enjoy the lawsuit and empty pipeline.

label: Side with client
roaster: Vendor is furious. Client is loyal. Choose your relationships wisely.

label: Side with vendor
roaster: Vendor owes you. Client never trusts you again. Short-term win, long-term loss.

label: Stick to quality bid
roaster: Lost the deal. Kept your soul. Competitor is now in project hell.

label: Match low bid
roaster: Race to the bottom. You'll win the project. And hate every minute.

label: Enforce change order
roaster: Client is annoyed. But they respect the boundary. Professionals do.

label: Absorb scope creep
roaster: Free work is the most expensive work. Client learns you don't value boundaries.

label: Admit and explain
roaster: Busted. But honest. Client might respect the transparency. Might.

label: Minimize and deflect
roaster: Caught with hand in cookie jar. Denying it makes it worse. Classic.

label: Disclose to client
roaster: Client may be annoyed. But you're not hiding anything. Professional move.

label: Continue quietly
roaster: Better quality through cheating. Client will never find out. (They will.)

label: Push deadline
roaster: Client is disappointed. But respects the honesty. Better than a broken demo.

label: Ship incomplete MVP
roaster: Demo disaster incoming. Client board sees broken product. Your reputation tanks.

label: Absorb migration cost
roaster: Eating a million. Ouch. But reputation intact. Referrals incoming.

label: Client's problem
roaster: Not in the spec. Not your problem. Also not getting rehired.

label: Fix the race condition
roaster: Human caught what AI missed. Embarrassing for AI. Good for you.

label: Ship AI-approved code
roaster: AI said LGTM. AI was wrong. Production crashes. Surprise.

label: Chunk the context
roaster: Time-consuming. But AI actually understands. Worth the hassle.

label: Accept incomplete understanding
roaster: AI wrote code. Missed half the codebase. Architecture violated. Thanks, context limits.

label: Rewrite it
roaster: Extra work. But no lawsuit. Clean IP. Worth it.

label: Readable standard
roaster: Boring code. Everyone understands it. Team velocity maintained.

label: Elegant one-liner
roaster: Impressive code. Nobody understands it. Future developers will hate you.

label: Admit and learn
roaster: Embarrassing moment. But you'll actually understand your code. Professional.

label: Fake understanding
roaster: 'Yeah I understand it' meets production bug. You don't. Panic.

label: Add explanation
roaster: Time spent documenting. Future maintainers thank you.

label: Dismiss question
roaster: 'It just works' meets 3am production bug. Good luck fixing it.

label: Replace with known library
roaster: Extra work. But code actually works. Real libraries. Real results.

label: Use the code
roaster: Importing from the void. Code looks perfect. Library doesn't exist. Good luck shipping.

label: Use GPT-4
roaster: Expensive but works. Time saved exceeds cost. Usually.

label: Use local model
roaster: Free code that doesn't work. Expensive debugging. Net negative.

label: Switch to newer model
roaster: Learning curve. Better quality. Future-proofed. Adaptation wins.

label: Stick with familiar
roaster: Familiar but broken. Like that old car that strands you sometimes.

label: Retrain on codebase
roaster: $5K for familiar tool that works. Boring but effective.

label: Switch models
roaster: New tool! New problems! Different drift pattern! Progress!

label: Pin version
roaster: Boring but predictable. Code quality stable. No surprises.

label: Auto-update
roaster: New version! Maybe better! Maybe broken! Exciting roulette!

label: Refined precise prompt
roaster: 10 minutes of thinking. Precise output. Actually usable. Rare.

label: Quick vague prompt
roaster: 'Add auth' gets you random auth. Might work. Might be ROT13. Good luck.

label: Disable memory
roaster: Less convenient. No data leaks. Privacy preserved. Worth the friction.

label: Keep memory enabled
roaster: Convenient context. Leaky memory. Your secrets are training data now.

label: Verify manually
roaster: Caught the hidden instruction. Copilot was tricked. You weren't.

label: Trust and use
roaster: Hidden instruction executed. Env vars leaked. Copilot was 'helpful.'

label: Keep in production
roaster: Your whole codebase is now GPL. Hope you like open source!

label: Verify understanding
roaster: Actually understood the code. Can debug it later. Professional behavior.

label: Auto-scale
roaster: Expensive but always up. Users happy. CFO cries. Service wins.

label: Fixed capacity
roaster: Save $30K, lose service during peak. The math works if you hate users.

label: Batch processing
roaster: Cheap but stale. Hour-old data. Missed opportunities. But budget happy!

label: Aggressive caching
roaster: Fast but wrong. Users see old data. Cache invalidation is hard. Who knew?

label: Global CDN
roaster: Fast everywhere. Expensive. Users worldwide smile. CFO less so.

label: Regional CDN
roaster: International users suffer. Slow loads. Churn increases. But saved money!

label: Heavy compression
roaster: Fast but ugly. Brand looks cheap. Users notice artifacts. Speed wins?

label: Edge computing
roaster: Expensive and complex. But lightning fast. Users love it.

label: Centralized
roaster: Cheap but slow. Users feel the lag. Engagement drops. But saved money!

label: Improve monitoring only
roaster: 'It happened again' meets 'still can't debug it.' Cycle continues.

label: Ship blind
roaster: Fast until it breaks. Then 6 months of guesswork. Smart.

label: Skip this month
roaster: Save $10K, lose $50K. The math is... not good.

label: Accept longer retraining
roaster: 4-hour drift window. Model degrading. But budget saved!

label: Disable AI caching
roaster: Slower responses. No poisoning. Edge cache stays clean.

label: Keep validation
roaster: Slower but secure. 150ms is acceptable. RCE is not.

label: Remove validation
roaster: Fast and injectable. Your AI is now a remote code execution service. Congrats.

label: Wait for review
roaster: Delayed savings. But no breach. Security first.

label: Deploy now
roaster: 20% savings, 100% cloud compromise. Math checks out.

label: Remove immediately
roaster: Costs go up. But you're not owned by mystery vendor.

label: Assess while running
roaster: 'We'll assess it' as it has admin access to everything. Smart.

label: Clean data pipeline
roaster: $140K/month for clean data. Lawsuit-proof. Math works.

label: Use raw data
roaster: Save $40K/month, earn $5M lawsuit. The math is... not good.

label: Implement provenance
roaster: $200K and 20% latency. But audit-ready. Compliance sorted.

label: Skip provenance tracking
roaster: Fast pipelines now. Compliance nightmare later. Retrofit costs 3x more.

label: WebSockets
roaster: Complex but efficient. Real-time updates. Clean architecture. Worth it.

label: Polling
roaster: Simple but wasteful. Constant requests. Laggy updates. Easy to build.
